name: Enochian Magic
description: Enochian magic is a complex system of ceremonial magic based on the 16th-century writings of John Dee and Edward Kelley, two prominent figures in Elizabethan England. Proponents claim it involves communication with angels and the manipulation of powerful forces through a unique language and set of symbols revealed to Dee and Kelley. The system purports to access a higher realm of spiritual knowledge and power, enabling practitioners to influence the material world. Its origins lie in Dee's pursuit of divine wisdom through scrying and spirit communication, facilitated by Kelley's purported ability to see and interpret angelic visions. Key evidence cited by proponents includes the extensive diaries and manuscripts of Dee and Kelley, detailing their angelic encounters, the Enochian language, and the intricate system of tables and sigils. Mainstream scholarship generally views Enochian magic as a product of Dee and Kelley's intellectual pursuits, influenced by Renaissance Neoplatonism, Hermeticism, and Christian Kabbalah. Skeptics often attribute Kelley's visions to fraud or psychological factors, and the Enochian language is considered by linguists to be a constructed language with no demonstrable connection to ancient or divine sources. The system gained significant popularity in the late 19th and 20th centuries through its adoption and adaptation by occultists such as the Hermetic Order of the Golden Dawn and Aleister Crowley, further shaping its modern interpretation and practice.
subtitle: Angelic language and ceremonial magic system

claims.claim: Enochian is a genuine angelic language.
claims.evidence: Dee and Kelley claimed it was revealed to them by angels.
claims.criticism: Linguists consider it a constructed language with no demonstrable historical or divine origin.

claims.claim: Enochian magic allows communication with angels and manipulation of spiritual forces.
claims.evidence: Dee and Kelley's diaries detail their supposed angelic communications and magical operations.
claims.criticism: Mainstream science dismisses the possibility of communicating with angels or manipulating spiritual forces through magical rituals.

claims.claim: Enochian magic unlocks access to higher realms of spiritual knowledge.
claims.evidence: Proponents claim the system provides insights into the nature of reality and the divine.
claims.criticism: Skeptics view such claims as subjective interpretations of personal experiences or elaborate fantasies.

keyFigures.name: John Dee
keyFigures.role: Primary researcher and recorder of the Enochian system

keyFigures.name: Edward Kelley
keyFigures.role: Scryer and purported medium for angelic communication

keyFigures.name: Aleister Crowley
keyFigures.role: Influential occultist who adapted and popularized Enochian magic

timeline.event: John Dee and Edward Kelley begin their Enochian workings.
The Hermetic Order of the Golden Dawn incorporates Enochian magic into its curriculum.
Aleister Crowley further develops and promotes Enochian magic in his own system of Thelema.

sources.author: Geoffrey James
sources.work: The Enochian Magick of Dr. John Dee
sources.title: Geoffrey James
